novel_features.name: Launch-day tracker
novel_features.description: Renders your launch's votes-over-time trajectory side-by-side with today's top 5 launches — the answer to 'am I catching up to the leader.' Sync-driven, refreshes from the local store.
novel_features.rationale: Indie founders refresh the leaderboard every few minutes during launch day. Only a CLI accumulating periodic snapshots can plot trajectories at all; PH's GraphQL is point-in-time and the website never charts trajectory.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this on launch day when a maker asks 'how am I tracking vs the leaders' — the side-by-side trajectories replace ten tabs.

novel_features.name: Hour-by-hour benchmark
novel_features.description: Reports percentile curves at hour-N for top-10 and top-50 launches in a topic, computed from accumulated local history. Tells a founder if their hour-6 votes are 'good' for their category.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local store of past launches with periodic snapshots. PH exposes only current votes; benchmarks across a category require accumulated history that doesn't exist anywhere else.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before launching to set realistic targets, or during launch to know whether a slow start is normal for the category or a real problem.

novel_features.name: Vote trajectory
novel_features.description: Plots a single launch's votes-over-time from local snapshots. Foundational for launch-day-tracker; also useful standalone for retro analysis after the fact.
novel_features.rationale: PH's GraphQL is point-in-time. Trajectory only exists if you've been syncing; the local store turns a stream of snapshots into a time series.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when reviewing a past launch or competitor — the curve shows momentum that a single end-of-day vote count hides.

novel_features.name: Comments → genuine questions
novel_features.description: Surfaces only comments that look like real questions (regex `?` plus heuristic verbs like 'how does', 'what's the', 'can it'), ranked by vote count. Cuts hundreds of launch-day comments down to the ones that need a maker's reply.
novel_features.rationale: Requires comments to be in the local store and a question-detection heuristic over comment bodies. PH's UI shows comments chronologically with no question filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use during or after launch day to identify which comments deserve a real reply versus which are cheerleading or spam.

novel_features.name: Side-by-side launch compare
novel_features.description: Column-aligned comparison of two or more launches: votes, comments, topics, tagline, url, launch-time delta. Replaces juggling browser tabs.
novel_features.rationale: Comparison across N posts requires N GraphQL fetches plus a layout step; no competing tool exposes it as a single command.
novel_features.example: producthunt posts compare cursor-ide windsurf-ide claude-code --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when a founder is benchmarking their launch against precedents or a marketer is triangulating between similar competitive launches.

novel_features.name: Category snapshot
novel_features.description: Slide-deck-ready brief for a topic over a window: leaderboard + momentum delta vs prior window + most active poster handles + top emerging tagline tags.
novel_features.rationale: Requires day-by-day local aggregations across `posts` filtered by `topic`, plus comparison against the prior window. A single GraphQL call returns current state, not the multi-window comparison.
novel_features.example: producthunt category snapshot --topic artificial-intelligence --window weekly --agent --select leaderboard,momentum_delta
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this on weekly category-research cadence — the single-output brief replaces opening 30 launch pages by hand.

novel_features.name: Brand-mention grep
novel_features.description: Searches taglines and descriptions of launches in a window for a term — your brand, a competitor's brand, a category keyword. Returns matching launches with the matched snippet.
novel_features.rationale: Requires synced launches and FTS5 over tagline+description. PH's UI has no keyword search across launches.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this as a recurring brand-mention monitor or to find competitive launches that name your category in their pitch.

novel_features.name: Lookalike launches
novel_features.description: Given a launch slug, finds the most similar prior launches by topic overlap plus tagline FTS rank. Builds a competitive set automatically.
novel_features.rationale: Requires both topic-set comparison and FTS rank over taglines, both of which need the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to build a competitive set quickly or to find precedent launches when planning your own positioning.

novel_features.name: Launches calendar
novel_features.description: Shows what launched what day in a week (and prior weeks for context), with hour-of-day distribution. Helps a founder pick a strong launch slot.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local store with launch timestamps and a calendar-rendering step. PH's UI organizes by day but doesn't surface hour-of-day or week-over-week density.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before scheduling a launch to find a less-crowded day or hour in your topic.

novel_features.name: Topic watch
novel_features.description: Detects new posts crossing a vote threshold in a topic since the last sync. Synthesizes an offline subscription against an API that has none.
novel_features.rationale: Product Hunt has no subscription/webhook endpoint. We synthesize one by diffing the local store between syncs and reporting only the deltas.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Schedule this in cron to alert on notable new launches in a vertical without hammering the GraphQL endpoint.

novel_features.name: Time-window posts query
novel_features.description: Local-first time-window query: `posts since 2h`, `posts since 24h`. Falls through to live GraphQL if the window extends past the last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the local store to know when it last synced and a fall-through path to `posts(postedAfter:)` for the gap. Single GraphQL calls cannot answer 'what changed since X' efficiently when X is recent.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this from agentic flows that ask 'what's new on Product Hunt' — the local-first behavior keeps token costs low and the fall-through guarantees freshness.

novel_features.name: Agent context snapshot
novel_features.description: Returns a single JSON blob covering top posts in a window, top comments, topic followers, and your viewer status. One call answers 'what's the state of this topic right now' for an agent.
novel_features.rationale: Folds 4 separate GraphQL calls into one local read. Only possible because the underlying data is already in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as the first call in an agentic Product Hunt workflow — one snapshot replaces 'list posts then list comments then check viewer'.

narrative.headline: Read Product Hunt from your terminal — works token-free for the daily skim, unlocks a launch-day cockpit and a marketer research desk in one onboarding step.
narrative.value_prop: A two-tier CLI for Product Hunt. The public Atom feed works with zero setup. A single `producthunt auth onboard` walks you through generating a free personal developer token (callback-URL trick included) to unlock the full GraphQL surface — posts, topics, collections, comments, viewer — plus the launch-day cockpit (trajectories, benchmarks, side-by-side compare, comment question-triage) and the marketer research desk (category snapshot, brand-mention grep, lookalike, launch calendar) that PH's UI has never offered.
narrative.auth_narrative: Product Hunt's GraphQL API supports two auth modes; the CLI handles both. Recommended for personal use: visit https://www.producthunt.com/v2/oauth/applications, create an application (the redirect URL field is required by the form but unused for personal-token flow — set it to `https://localhost/callback`), then scroll to the bottom of the app page and click `Create Token` to generate a developer token that never expires. Set `PRODUCT_HUNT_TOKEN=<your-token>` or run `producthunt auth onboard` for an interactive walkthrough. For CI/automation, the alternate mode is OAuth `client_credentials`: set `PRODUCT_HUNT_CLIENT_ID` and `PRODUCT_HUNT_CLIENT_SECRET` from the same app page; the CLI exchanges them for an access token internally and refreshes on 401 (note: under OAuth client_credentials, the `whoami` command returns null because the public scope has no user context). The public Atom feed (`producthunt feed`) needs no token at all.

quickstart.comment: Token-free first read — the public Atom feed surfaces the latest 5 launches
Interactive setup for the personal developer token (includes the callback URL trick)
Confirms the token works and shows your remaining complexity-points budget
GraphQL-backed snapshot of today's top launches with votes, comments, and topics
Full detail for a single launch by slug — agent-friendly JSON
Backfill the local store so trajectories, benchmarks, and offline search work

troubleshoots.fix: Run `producthunt doctor` — if the token is missing run `producthunt auth onboard`; if the token is present but rejected, regenerate it at https://www.producthunt.com/v2/oauth/applications and `producthunt auth set-token <new>`

troubleshoots.symptom: Maker names and commenter usernames show as `[REDACTED]`
troubleshoots.fix: Product Hunt globally redacts `Post.makers`, `Post.comments[].user`, `user()` non-self lookups, and `Collection.user` for both auth modes — `Post.user` (the poster) and `viewer` (yourself) are unredacted; pass `--select` to suppress the [REDACTED] columns
You are using OAuth client_credentials; the public scope has no user context — switch to a developer token (`producthunt auth onboard` will do this) to use `whoami`
PH's per-token complexity budget is 6,250 points / 15 min — `producthunt whoami` reports the remaining budget and reset epoch; rerun after the reset or split the work across windows

troubleshoots.symptom: RSS feed entries are missing votes / comments / makers
troubleshoots.fix: Product Hunt's Atom feed is intentionally minimal — `producthunt auth onboard` unlocks the GraphQL tier where those fields exist

narrative.when_to_use: Pick this CLI when an agent or script needs Product Hunt data — daily launch leaderboards, topic-vertical scouting, individual launch detail with comments, or aggregated trajectories that PH's own UI never exposes. The local SQLite store and GraphQL fall-through mean repeated queries stay cheap; the no-auth feed tier means the CLI is useful before the user has even configured a key. Indie founders launching this week reach for the launch-day cockpit (`launch-day`, `benchmark`, `trajectory`, `questions`, `compare`); marketers doing competitive research reach for the marketer research desk (`category snapshot`, `posts grep`, `lookalike`, `launches calendar`).

recipes.title: Launch-day cockpit for your own launch
recipes.explanation: Renders your launch's vote trajectory side-by-side with today's top 5 — the answer to 'am I catching up to the leader' without refreshing the website

recipes.title: Triage a launch's comments to genuine questions
recipes.explanation: Filters synced comments to only those that look like real questions (regex `\\?` plus question-verbs) ranked by vote count — replaces scrolling 200 comments to find the 5 that deserve a reply

recipes.title: Narrow a deeply nested response with --agent --select
recipes.explanation: Posts list responses are deeply nested (edges -> node -> user); pairing `--agent` with dotted `--select` paths keeps the output to just the columns the agent needs and slashes token cost

recipes.title: Weekly category brief for marketing
recipes.explanation: Single-output brief: leaderboard for the week, momentum delta vs prior week, most active poster handles, top emerging tagline tags — slide-deck-ready

recipes.title: Brand-mention monitor
recipes.explanation: Finds any launch in the window with `claude` or `anthropic` in tagline or description — schedule it nightly to catch competitive activity

trigger_phrases: what launched on product hunt today
find ai launches on product hunt this week
how is my product hunt launch tracking
summarize the comments on this product hunt post
what does a good product hunt launch look like at hour 6
